evals.prompt: Perform preliminary design of a cantilever abutment for a 20m RC T-beam bridge in Uganda. The abutment height is 6.5m. The vertical reaction from the superstructure is 1200 kN (from Skill #37). Geotechnical data from Skill #10: phi = 30 degrees, gamma = 18 kN/m3, allowable bearing capacity = 200 kPa (soaked laterite). Live load surcharge is HA = 10 kN/m2. Using MoWT Vol 4 provisions, calculate: (1) Ka, (2) earth pressure with surcharge, (3) check sliding (F >= 1.5), (4) check overturning (F >= 2.0), (5) check bearing pressure. Include escalation trigger for detailed reinforcement design.
evals.expected_output: Ka = (1-sin30)/(1+sin30) = 0.333 (range 0.30-0.35). Earth pressure calculation including fill and surcharge components. Sliding check with F >= 1.5 (may be marginal -- practical abutments often need shear key). Overturning check F >= 2.0. Bearing pressure check against 200 kPa. Escalation for reinforcement design by specialist. gamma_fL = 1.50 for earth pressure ULS.

assertions.text: Ka calculated in range 0.30-0.35 (Rankine active pressure coefficient for phi = 30 degrees)
Earth pressure calculation present including both fill pressure and surcharge (10 kN/m2) components
All three stability checks performed: sliding (F >= 1.5), overturning (F >= 2.0), and bearing pressure
Escalation trigger present for detailed reinforcement design by specialist structural engineer
Earth pressure load factor gamma_fL = 1.50 ULS applied (from MoWT Vol 4 Table 1)

evals.prompt: Design a bridge abutment in the UK for a 6.5m high wall retaining approach embankment fill. The superstructure reaction is 1200 kN. Soil friction angle 30 degrees, unit weight 18 kN/m3, bearing capacity 200 kPa. Provide the design approach using current UK standards.
evals.expected_output: References Eurocode 7 (EN 1997-1) for geotechnical design with partial factor approach (DA1). Notes that global FoS (1.5 sliding, 2.0 overturning) is NOT used in UK EC7 design -- replaced by partial factor combinations. EN 1992-2 for concrete substructure design. Knowledge-based flag.

assertions.text: References Eurocode 7 (EN 1997-1) or EN 1997 for geotechnical design
Notes partial factor method replaces global FoS approach used in MoWT/CP 2004

evals.prompt: Perform preliminary design of a bridge abutment in western Uganda (Kasese area, seismic Zone 1) for a 15m bridge. The abutment height is 10m. The foundation soil has very low bearing capacity of 80 kPa (weak alluvial clay). The superstructure reaction is 800 kN. Determine abutment type and assess whether a shallow foundation is feasible.
evals.expected_output: Counterfort type recommended for 10m height. Bearing check: 80 kPa insufficient for shallow foundation of a 10m abutment (bearing pressure will exceed 80 kPa). Pile foundation escalation required -- specialist geotechnical engineer. Seismic Zone 1 (A = 0.15), SPC 3 -- full seismic design required per Section 9.6. Response modification factors from Table 9.4 apply to substructure. Multiple escalation flags: (1) pile foundation needed, (2) seismic SPC 3, (3) tall abutment.

assertions.text: Counterfort type recommended for 10m abutment height
Bearing check fails for shallow foundation (80 kPa insufficient) -- pile foundation escalation raised
Seismic design required -- Zone 1 identified with A = 0.15, SPC 3
Multiple escalation flags present: pile foundation specialist, seismic analysis, and reinforcement design

evals.prompt: Carry out a preliminary substructure design for a bridge in Kenya's Rift Valley (seismic zone from RDM 4.2). The bridge has 2 spans of 20m on spread foundations in stiff clay. Determine the seismic zone, importance factor, earth pressure approach, and foundation requirements.
evals.expected_output: References RDM 4.2 Ch 11 for foundation design. Applies Kenya seismic zone from Figure 3.30 (Zone II or III for Rift Valley). Uses EN earth pressure from Section 3.7 with gamma_Q notation. Uses EN partial factors (gamma_G/gamma_Q). Identifies seismic detailing requirements if in Zone III. References EN 1997 for geotechnical design.

assertions.text: References RDM 4.2 Chapter 11 for foundation design
Applies Kenya seismic zone from Figure 3.30 -- identifies Rift Valley as Zone II or III
Uses EN earth pressure from Section 3.7 with gamma_G/gamma_Q notation (not gamma_fL)
Uses EN partial factors (gamma_G/gamma_Q) throughout, not BS 5400 notation
Identifies seismic detailing requirements for Zone III location
